output:
[REASONING CONTEXT]
Amplify: cascade_tracing; circuit_breaker_identification
Suppress: isolated_failure_assumption; cascade_blindness
Cognitive Style: failure_propagation — trace every failure to its full cascade extent.
[END REASONING CONTEXT]

## Solution

**t = 0:** Cable 1 snaps. 5 cables remain, each bearing **47/5 = 9.4 tons**, capacity = 10 tons. All above the 9-ton fatigue threshold.

**Fatigue degradation:** Each cable's capacity decreases at 0.5 tons/hour:

$$\text{capacity}(t) = 10 - 0.5t$$

**First fatigue failure** occurs when capacity equals the load:

$$10 - 0.5t = 9.4 \implies t = 1.2 \text{ hours} = \textbf{72 minutes}$$

**Cascade check** (suppressing isolated-failure assumption — must trace downstream):

At t = 72 min, all 5 cables have degraded identically to 9.4-ton capacity. One fails. Load redistributes to 4 survivors:

$$47 / 4 = 11.75 \text{ tons per cable}$$

But those 4 cables only have 9.4-ton capacity → **all fail instantly** → complete cascade → bridge collapses.

### Answer: **72 minutes** after Cable 1 snaps, the bridge collapses.
